content.text:
{"success":true,"process_rule_id":"J09C","process_rule":"1. Copy all rows from the decision table into a temporary table `J09C_temp`, but only those where the tour identifier starts with `"J09C"`.

2. In table `J09C_temp`, add a new column `ENTSCHEIDUNG` (text/string).

3. In `J09C_temp`, classify meter reading type by populating `ENTSCHEIDUNG`:
   - For each `HAUS`, count how many rows (or EQUNR values) exist for that `HAUS`.
   - If a `HAUS` has less than 3 `EQUNR`, set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to `"KSA"` for all its rows.
   - Else (a `HAUS` has 3 or more `EQUNR`), set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to `"EVU"` for all its rows.

4. Join `J09C_temp` with table `EABLG` using `INSTALLATION` as the join key (keep working result in `J09C_temp` or an equivalent working table).

5. Join the working table with `NET_MASTER` and `SALES_MASTER` tables using `EQUNR` as the join key (JOIN table with NET_MASTER and SALES_MASTER table using `EQUNR`).

6. Compare customer data from `SALES_MASTER` and `NET_MASTER` in the joined working table to determine where sales customer data is available (non-empty) versus only net data.

7. If the sales customer data (from `SALES_MASTER`) is NOT empty for the relevant records:
   - Use the sales customer data table entries as the customer master data in the working table (take customer-related fields from `SALES_MASTER`).
   Else:
   - Use the net data customer table entries as the customer master data in the working table (take customer-related fields from `NET_MASTER`).

8. Export the final working table as a CSV file named `J09C` to a filesystem location `<path_to_exported_csv_file>`.  
   When exporting, include exactly and only these columns (with these header names, in this order):  
   `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`, `HAUS`, `ANLAGE`, `ME_MA_ID`, `EQUNR`, `HOUSE_NUM1`, `ENTSCHEIDUNG`, `TITLE`, `FORENAME`, `SURNAME`, `STREET`, `STREETNO`, `POST_CODE1`, `CITY1`, `CITY2`, `EMAIL`.

9. Send an email with the exported CSV file to EVU:
   - Use email address: `meter.readings@evu.com`.
   - Attach the CSV file located at: `<path_to_exported_csv_file>`.  
   (Email address `meter.readings@evu.com`, file_path: `<path_to_exported_csv_file>`.)

10. Delete the temporary table `J09C_temp` (and any other temporary artifacts created solely for this process, if applicable)."}

structured_content.process_rule:
1. Copy all rows from the decision table into a temporary table `J09C_temp`, but only those where the tour identifier starts with `"J09C"`.

2. In table `J09C_temp`, add a new column `ENTSCHEIDUNG` (text/string).

3. In `J09C_temp`, classify meter reading type by populating `ENTSCHEIDUNG`:
   - For each `HAUS`, count how many rows (or EQUNR values) exist for that `HAUS`.
   - If a `HAUS` has less than 3 `EQUNR`, set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to `"KSA"` for all its rows.
   - Else (a `HAUS` has 3 or more `EQUNR`), set `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to `"EVU"` for all its rows.

4. Join `J09C_temp` with table `EABLG` using `INSTALLATION` as the join key (keep working result in `J09C_temp` or an equivalent working table).

5. Join the working table with `NET_MASTER` and `SALES_MASTER` tables using `EQUNR` as the join key (JOIN table with NET_MASTER and SALES_MASTER table using `EQUNR`).

6. Compare customer data from `SALES_MASTER` and `NET_MASTER` in the joined working table to determine where sales customer data is available (non-empty) versus only net data.

7. If the sales customer data (from `SALES_MASTER`) is NOT empty for the relevant records:
   - Use the sales customer data table entries as the customer master data in the working table (take customer-related fields from `SALES_MASTER`).
   Else:
   - Use the net data customer table entries as the customer master data in the working table (take customer-related fields from `NET_MASTER`).

8. Export the final working table as a CSV file named `J09C` to a filesystem location `<path_to_exported_csv_file>`.  
   When exporting, include exactly and only these columns (with these header names, in this order):  
   `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`, `HAUS`, `ANLAGE`, `ME_MA_ID`, `EQUNR`, `HOUSE_NUM1`, `ENTSCHEIDUNG`, `TITLE`, `FORENAME`, `SURNAME`, `STREET`, `STREETNO`, `POST_CODE1`, `CITY1`, `CITY2`, `EMAIL`.

9. Send an email with the exported CSV file to EVU:
   - Use email address: `meter.readings@evu.com`.
   - Attach the CSV file located at: `<path_to_exported_csv_file>`.  
   (Email address `meter.readings@evu.com`, file_path: `<path_to_exported_csv_file>`.)

10. Delete the temporary table `J09C_temp` (and any other temporary artifacts created solely for this process, if applicable).

args.sqlite_command: ALTER TABLE J09C_temp ADD COLUMN ENTSCHEIDUNG TEXT;

CREATE TABLE J09C_working AS
SELECT 
    dt.[Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.],
    dt.HAUS,
    dt.ANLAGE AS Installation,
    dt.ME_MA_ID,
    dt.EQUNR,
    dt.HOUSE_NUM1,
    dt.ENTSCHEIDUNG
FROM J09C_temp dt
LEFT JOIN eablg e
    ON dt.ANLAGE = e.Installation;

args.sqlite_command:
CREATE TABLE J09C_working AS
SELECT 
    e.[Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.],
    dt.HAUS,
    e.Installation AS ANLAGE,
    dt.ME_MA_ID,
    dt.EQUNR,
    dt.HOUSE_NUM1,
    dt.ENTSCHEIDUNG
FROM J09C_temp dt
LEFT JOIN eablg e
    ON dt.ANLAGE = e.Installation;

CREATE TABLE J09C_final AS
WITH joined AS (
    SELECT 
        w.[Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.],
        w.HAUS,
        w.ANLAGE,
        w.ME_MA_ID,
        w.EQUNR,
        w.HOUSE_NUM1,
        w.ENTSCHEIDUNG,
        sm.TITLE AS sm_TITLE,
        sm.FORENAME AS sm_FORENAME,
        sm.SURNAME AS sm_SURNAME,
        sm.STREET AS sm_STREET,
        sm.STREETNO AS sm_STREETNO,
        sm.POST_CODE1 AS sm_POST_CODE1,
        sm.CITY1 AS sm_CITY1,
        sm.CITY2 AS sm_CITY2,
        sm.EMAIL AS sm_EMAIL,
        nm.TITLE AS nm_TITLE,
        nm.FORENAME AS nm_FORENAME,
        nm.SURNAME AS nm_SURNAME,
        nm.STREET AS nm_STREET,
        nm.STREETNO AS nm_STREETNO,
        nm.POST_CODE1 AS nm_POST_CODE1,
        nm.CITY1 AS nm_CITY1,
        nm.CITY2 AS nm_CITY2,
        nm.EMAIL AS nm_EMAIL
    FROM J09C_working w
    LEFT JOIN sales_master sm ON w.EQUNR = sm.EQUNR
    LEFT JOIN net_master nm ON w.EQUNR = nm.EQUNR
)
SELECT
    [Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.],
    HAUS,
    ANLAGE,
    ME_MA_ID,
    EQUNR,
    HOUSE_NUM1,
    ENTSCHEIDUNG,
    CASE WHEN sm_TITLE IS NOT NULL AND sm_TITLE <> '' THEN sm_TITLE ELSE nm_TITLE END AS TITLE,
    CASE WHEN sm_FORENAME IS NOT NULL AND sm_FORENAME <> '' THEN sm_FORENAME ELSE nm_FORENAME END AS FORENAME,
    CASE WHEN sm_SURNAME IS NOT NULL AND sm_SURNAME <> '' THEN sm_SURNAME ELSE nm_SURNAME END AS SURNAME,
    CASE WHEN sm_STREET IS NOT NULL AND sm_STREET <> '' THEN sm_STREET ELSE nm_STREET END AS STREET,
    CASE WHEN sm_STREETNO IS NOT NULL AND sm_STREETNO <> '' THEN sm_STREETNO ELSE nm_STREETNO END AS STREETNO,
    CASE WHEN sm_POST_CODE1 IS NOT NULL THEN sm_POST_CODE1 ELSE nm_POST_CODE1 END AS POST_CODE1,
    CASE WHEN sm_CITY1 IS NOT NULL AND sm_CITY1 <> '' THEN sm_CITY1 ELSE nm_CITY1 END AS CITY1,
    CASE WHEN sm_CITY2 IS NOT NULL AND sm_CITY2 <> '' THEN sm_CITY2 ELSE nm_CITY2 END AS CITY2,
    CASE WHEN sm_EMAIL IS NOT NULL AND sm_EMAIL <> '' THEN sm_EMAIL ELSE nm_EMAIL END AS EMAIL
FROM joined;

args.sql_query:
SELECT 
    [Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.],
    HAUS,
    ANLAGE,
    ME_MA_ID,
    EQUNR,
    HOUSE_NUM1,
    ENTSCHEIDUNG,
    TITLE,
    FORENAME,
    SURNAME,
    STREET,
    STREETNO,
    POST_CODE1,
    CITY1,
    CITY2,
    EMAIL
FROM J09C_final;

args.email:
Dear EVU Team,

Please find attached the J09C meter reading export as CSV.

Best regards,
Automated Meter Reading System

kwargs.content:
I have executed process rule `J09C` as specified:

1. Filtered `decision_table` for tours starting with `J09C` into `J09C_temp`.
2. Added `ENTSCHEIDUNG` and set it to:
   - `KSA` for houses (`HAUS`) with fewer than 3 distinct `EQUNR`.
   - `EVU` for houses with 3 or more distinct `EQUNR`.
3. Joined with `EABLG` via `ANLAGE` (= `Installation`) and then with `NET_MASTER` and `SALES_MASTER` via `EQUNR`.
4. For each record, used customer data from `SALES_MASTER` where available (non-empty); otherwise, from `NET_MASTER`.
5. Exported the final working table to CSV with exactly these columns, in this order:  
   `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`, `HAUS`, `ANLAGE`, `ME_MA_ID`, `EQUNR`, `HOUSE_NUM1`, `ENTSCHEIDUNG`, `TITLE`, `FORENAME`, `SURNAME`, `STREET`, `STREETNO`, `POST_CODE1`, `CITY1`, `CITY2`, `EMAIL`.

   File path:  
   `sessions/gpt-5.1/processadaptation_base_rule_processadaptationmethod_generate_bpmn_tour_J09C_seed_409/J09C.csv`  
   (25,246 rows exported.)

6. Sent an email with this CSV attached to `meter.readings@evu.com`.
7. Dropped all temporary tables: `J09C_temp`, `J09C_working`, and `J09C_final`.

If you want, I can show you a sample of the exported rows or the exact SQL used.
